tool.name: Phase C.2 triage
tool.description: flag Liftoff-projected genes that need CESAR2 fallback
tool.command: ## Galaxy stores datasets as .dat; pyfaidx needs a real .fasta to index alongside. ln -s '$query_fasta' query.fasta && mkdir -p outdir && python '$__tool_directory__/phase_c2_triage.py' --liftoff-gff '$liftoff_gff' --query-fasta query.fasta --reference-bed '$reference_bed' --query-name '$query_name' --output-dir outdir --core-identity-min $core_identity_min --core-coverage-min $core_coverage_min --family-identity-min $family_identity_min --subtelomere-bp $subtelomere_bp #if str($family_list) != 'None' --family-list '$family_list' #end if --log-level INFO

param.label: Liftoff GFF3
param.help: Projected gene annotation from Liftoff. Genes must carry the attributes the triage rules read (sequence_ID, coverage, extra_copy_number, valid_ORFs, partial_mapping).

param.label: Query genome FASTA
param.help: The query assembly the GFF coordinates refer to. Seqids must match the GFF chrom column.

param.label: Reference BED12
param.help: Reference gene models in BED format. Column 4 (name) must match the (normalized) gene IDs so flagged genes can be emitted into needs_cesar2.bed.

validator.message: Letters, digits, underscore, hyphen, dot only

param.label: Gene family list (optional)
param.help: Optional TSV (gene_id, family) of known variant-gene families (SICAvar, PIR, ...). When supplied, rule R8 forces those genes to CESAR2 fallback and relaxes the identity threshold.

param.label: Core gene minimum identity
param.help: Rule R2 threshold for non-family genes.

param.label: Family gene minimum identity
param.help: Rule R2 threshold for genes on the family list.

param.label: Subtelomere flank (bp)
param.help: Rule R7: genes within this distance of a chromosome end are flagged.

tool.help:
**Phase C.2 triage**
Decides, for each gene projected onto a query assembly by [Liftoff](https://github.com/agshumate/Liftoff), whether the projection is clean enough to keep or whether it should be re-aligned with CESAR2 / TOGA2 in the next phase of the *P. knowlesi* orthology pipeline.
Eight rules are evaluated per gene:
- **R1** frame disruption (`valid_ORFs` flag, CDS length not a multiple of 3, internal stop codon) - **R2** sequence identity below threshold (core 0.95, family 0.85) - **R3** reference CDS coverage below threshold (default 0.90) - **R4** copy-number variation for non-family genes - **R5** partial mapping flagged by Liftoff - **R6** non-canonical splice sites (only GT-AG / AT-AC accepted) - **R7** subtelomeric location (within the flank distance of a chromosome end) - **R8** known family membership (when a family list is supplied) - forces fallback
-----
**Inputs**
- *Liftoff GFF3* - projected annotation; genes are read for `sequence_ID`, `coverage`, `extra_copy_number`, `valid_ORFs`, and `partial_mapping`. - *Query genome FASTA* - the assembly the GFF coordinates refer to. - *Reference BED12* - reference models; column 4 names must match gene IDs. - *Gene family list* - optional TSV (`gene_id`, `family`) enabling rule R8.
**Outputs**
- `triage.tsv` - per-gene decision and triggered rules. - `needs_cesar2.bed` - reference BED lines for flagged genes. - `liftoff_clean.gff3` - the input GFF filtered to non-flagged genes. - `summary.json` - counts, per-rule tallies, and the overall `fallback_rate`.
-----
Ported from `Pv4-pangenome/v3/pipeline/impl/scripts/phase_c2_triage.py`.
